test: Attention-variant microbench (pure torch, 1x RTX 5090 sm_120) + GraphRunner re-validation, to pick the CUDA-graph masked-read approach by measurement. Verdict: CUDA-graph is NOT worth it on current torch.

attention_op_graphed_ms.comment: graphed = launch overhead removed = the in-graph compute cost. flash :total is the floor.
attention_op_graphed_ms.takeaway: the bucketed read (:alen + mask) can't match flash :total; manual matmul is the fastest GRAPHABLE bucketed variant (beats SDPA-additive 3-4x AND flex here). But attention is a tiny slice of the block, so the variant barely matters for the block graph.

graphrunner_with_manual.vs_eager_causal: ~4-10 (manual vs flash bf16 drift, amplified by MoE — expected, accepted as a self-consistent decode kernel)

root_cause: CUDA-graph removes per-kernel LAUNCH overhead. torch 2.10+cu128 had high launch overhead -> the fixed-position probe hit 3.40x. torch 2.11+cu130 already eliminated it (eager block ~3ms) -> graph nets only ~1.05x, and the masked-read overhead eats that. `pip install vllm` now pulls torch 2.11+, so real deployments see ~1.05x.
decision: ABANDON CUDA-graph as the throughput lever (not worth the complexity + non-bit-identical decode for ~5%). M25_CUDA_GRAPH stays opt-in/default-OFF, documented for high-launch-overhead torch only. The GraphRunner machinery is proven-faithful and left isolated.
redirect: The throughput bottleneck on current torch is NOT GPU launches — GPU is ~0.75ms/layer (~10ms/13-layer stage). Next: (1) PROFILE a warm ring to split GPU vs WAN-RTT vs handoff vs draft per traversal; (2) attack the real lever — most likely DRAFTING (raise g = tokens/traversal, the WAN-amortization lever) since the WAN round dominates, not the GPU.
